title: Talking About 24 hours of Moab Solo Mountain Bike Race 2010 - 1stx 2/4

transcript.text: And then on the uh my fourth lap I don't have my tripod so I have to like on my fourth lap
Um about three quarters of the way through the lead guy passes me and I don't know how I
Recognized him I just knew it was him because I saw the youtube video of him talking from the
Year before and I just even even though I looked at him from like a 90 degree angle I could
Recognize him because he had I guess kelly mcgalky I guess he has like distinctive eyes or something
But yeah he zooms by me like I'm standing still man guy has not even started to slow down yet
After like how many hours of riding like five hours or something I don't even know
And then a few minutes later at the beginning of the climb josh testado passes me and I recognize
Him because he had the national championship jersey on and I was looking at those guys before the race
Started and I go kelly's just fine one minute ahead of you but he didn't say anything I think he had
His walkman on I think he's the only guy in the whole thing there who I saw had their walkman on
I think yari kirkland may have had the walkman on though I can't remember and then um that was my
Third lap and then I did that was my fourth lap and then I did a fifth lap you know and I'm still
So I'm coming in and my family I made sure that my family would be like my mom and my two brothers
Would be like on the side of the course my my car they let the solo riders put their cars on the
North side of the course right next to the course um so that was cool so my car was only like
20 feet or 25 feet away from the course itself so they just walked up and they're like so every time
I came through they'd I'd make sure that they would get I would give them all the packages empty
Packages of my gels and then give them and then they would make sure that I had at least five gels
In my left pocket when I would go and then I would like um I would I usually would go through the
Whole technical section without doing anything without taking anything and um or maybe I would take
One before it started but I don't think I did and then I would just wait until because the
First half of it is technical like straight up straight down over rocks like really hard
And then the second half is just dirt roads and uh I was actually doing quite well in the technical
Section because I knew all the right lines because I'm heavy so that took me like down the gully and
The momentum back up the gully and I guess I was just I'm just a good climber even though I'm heavy
And the steep stuff but when once I got to the dirt road I was like getting my ass handed to me which is
Kind of interesting because my training consisted of zero mountain biking and I haven't mountain
Biked for years and all my training consisted of was bike touring so you'd think that I'd be good
On the flats but no I was getting my ass kicked on the flats and I was kind of keeping up with guys
Sort of at least on the downhill part of it on the technical part but um it's kind of funny because
When the teams come by especially as the as the day wore on like fat guys and girls and stuff would
Like be passing me at the beginning of the course like I'm standing still just boom rocking
Like really fat guys and then I would pass them like at the end of the course because they get tired
But um yeah that was like and then I did like a fifth lap and so I would wait and then I did like
All of my gels like on the dirt road like usually on the on the last climb and then I'd go and I'd eat
And then they my mom wanted me to have a well they asked me what I would have for actual food and
It was a turkey sandwich um I think maybe they put tomato in it and like mustard or something
But uh and then like on one lap on the night lap she had um a bunch of tomato soup and that was a
Really cool thing because it was heated and you know it got me warm and it was really delicious
And easy to go down and um and then like um mashed potatoes and that was a really good thing to eat
Too easy to eat and came around it down she she I mean we didn't even discuss that before but that
Was a really cool thing to cook for me um but anyways yeah so I did the fifth lap and then I did the
Sixth lap and um I uh got done with the technical section and um there was this guy behind me he
Starts talking he goes he goes yo man you mind if I just like follow you for the rest of the lap like
And follow your lines because people were like noticing that I had all the good lines in fact at
One point on a night ride some girl was like after she passed me she goes dude nice smooth lines man
So anyways I was like yeah sure and then he starts chatting me up he's like and it turns out he was
On the sixth lap as well so he was like from calgary canada and he's all some dude passes him he's like
How's it going guys like yeah we're just chit chatting away you know he wanted to like hang out
With me um he sounded like an older guy just from his voice like 50 but I never looked at him but then
Like all of a sudden I was like ow because my uh my inner thighs cramped up on me and he's like
What's wrong I was like dude cramped go ahead so he's like you know zooms off and um because like
When I did the mountain biking a couple weeks beforehand on that for three days my inner
Muscles like the muscle on the inner thigh I don't know what that's called um was totally sore
Like I and so I guess in mountain biking you use that that leg that muscle more than in road biking
Like maybe because your legs go out and in like butterfly or something from all the moving around
And turning and stuff but like but yeah they started spasming man cramping and it was really painful
But as soon as I would take off the um the pressure like I'd have to stop pedaling and then I would only
Like and they both were spasming but not both at the same time like it was mainly my left one so then
Like my left one would go and I would just kind of not do anything with my left leg and I would just
Pedal with my right leg but like really easily so that I wouldn't spasm too and then the right leg
Would spasm so then I would do the left leg but really easily and it was kind of like and I could
Feel it like spasming and cramping and everything while while it was moving around but it wasn't putting
Any pressure on it so it was kind of a good way to like loosen it up but yeah so then that happened for
Like a couple of miles but then it kind of I just took it easy and it kind of went away and I just
Made sure to eat like also I was having like five gels in a lap and um I was having um one packet of
Shot blocks um electrolyte and then I told my family to put two packets of emergency in each of my
Camelback waters and on one of the laps I drank all of my water I ran out of water but it was on the
Downhill because I tell them like you know only give me as much water as I've been drinking like
Don't give me too much but I think they were like filling up all the way but I noticed later that
They gave me like the little the little bag because I accidentally bought a little mini camelback
But I can't remember how much how much water that has I think it's like the same as like a big water
Bottle or something but um and I also took told them to put two scoops of perpetuum in there but I don't
Think they were doing that I only noticed perpetuum in one of the bladders that I emptied out today
But the other two had like none of that in and no trace of it so they must have forgotten or they
Didn't care but they'd put what put it in one of them um but um I uh so then um I got through there
Through that lap kind of having to pace my forced to pace myself um and then I got through
And then I did lap seven and then I did and then on lap eight right when I was done with the um
Technical part my left knee suddenly goes like I couldn't put any pressure on it it went out
Just like when I was in austria and um um because of because I had been cranking it sort of I'd been
Like pedaling at too too slow of cadence and I guess because of my weight 176 pounds and going
Over those climbs and like a low gear because I've been doing them in like the middle chain ring and
Stuff I'm just more of a I I ride at a slower a lower cadence than most people um because I for me
It feels like I can serve energy that way and I also ride out of the saddle in a really really low
Cadence like when I was um bike touring just to like make my butt feel better and just to
Um you know stretch out and stuff but I couldn't stand up because I was on a full suspension bike
And those things are really squishy like when I buy my own 29 inch mountain bike I'm not going to get a
Full suspension I'm going to get a hardtail because I'll be able to stand out of the saddle on that
And just crank it I think that's a smart for me that's a good thing to do I can go faster that way
I can put on the lowest the highest gear and just crank it and and use my body weight instead not like
Burn out my knee so anyways I did the rest of that lap um I had that's when I was like all right I had
To like mentally just program myself to spin at like really fast cadence like this like what a lot
Of the other people were doing but it kind of kind of worked out for me anyways because um you know
I was still able to ride as hard as I could as hard as my energy would allow at that point
And then uh so then I did lap nine and then lap 10 and um lap 11 and then with like seven hours to go
I remember I remember going at five in the morning um I I started getting as I started I got an asthma attack
Um and I would really like feel it um on the technical section so then I would at the end of
Hike a bike where you have to walk up hike it up the really steep part that sandy pit I I would get
Off the bike and do four hits of the inhaler and then I would feel like I didn't really have asthma
For the rest of the lap and then I would have or it wasn't really that bad and then I and then I
Would get the asthma again on the climb and and I think that's when I started feeling a lot of pain
On my butt like from the weight but I couldn't sit stand up because it was a full suspension bike
And then um on lap the beginning of lap 11 no no the beginning of lap 12 because I did 13 laps in
The at the end the beginning of lap 12 my second to last lap this was like my first daytime lap
My mom gives me a peanut butter sandwich
And um I already knew this but I had forgotten it that sometimes peanuts gives me asthma um I ate that
Peanut butter sandwich and like two minutes later I got fucking slammed and what time was this
Um I remember I started getting asthma at five because I remember thinking to myself oh fuck I have
Seven hours left with this fucking asthma this sucks but then but but then with like the beginning
With two hours left so that would have been like five hours to go at like um seven in the morning or
Something like that yeah that was the beginning of my second to last lap I got slammed with the worst
Fucking asthma attack with a horrendous asthma attack I could not breathe for shit I was breathing like
This it was like five percent of my lungs um yeah I had to take like three breaths a second
And um so then I stopped at the beginning of the climb as the as the thing turned to left off the
Road took four hits got back on didn't do shit went up hike a bike got off took like five more hits
Um went through that lap and it didn't get better I was fucked the whole time I got off the bike
I go through the lap that lap and I um at the at the end of lap 11
I took my first had my first bowel movement because the lap the lap before that I was eating and I noticed
When I was standing up I didn't notice when I was on the bike but I noticed when I was standing up
That I could have a bowel movement so I was like all right at the beginning of the next move
Bike lap I'll do that there they had the outhouses next to the uh where you do the uh your card every
Time you go through the lap the the lap you have to do your slap it down twice and you see the the
Like light turn green twice so anyways and then I did so I had my bowel movement and then I did the
Lap and then I and uh you know I felt like I wasn't really wasting time by doing that you know by
Like not holding it in for the rest of the ride because I had to pee anyways and I figured both
Of those would take about the same time but I went to um when I had my bowel movement I you know I went
To wipe but I couldn't because that's when I noticed that my butt was totally raw like you know where you
Sit down totally raw like you know like no more skin left type of raw and like you so I couldn't
Why I just could dab so then I was and and while I did that I I uh I cramped up my left lat muscle
And um so then I went through and anyway and then at the next lap I was I noticed I had diarrhea I was
Having diarrhea problems so I went in the next lap and so I had another dump two in two in a row
Two and my 11th lap or the beginning of my 12th lap and then the beginning of my 13th lap I had to do
It again two in a row and that was probably because most of the energy that I was taking was um just the
Gels it was like liquid energy or something but anyways I was in there in the outhouse and the announcer
Dude goes oh and here comes chris cannon I was like oh fuck now it's on because that was the
Second place girls and I didn't lap her so I knew she was behind me and she as soon as she came up
We were like tied I was like all right I can't let her beat me oops we gotta do another one
Okay
So
